scenes.text: In this lecture, we will explore the fundamental concepts of Docker, a platform that automates the deployment of applications inside lightweight, portable containers. We'll cover the main components of Docker, including images, containers, Dockerfiles, and the Docker Hub. By understanding how these elements work together, you'll gain insight into how Docker helps streamline development workflows and simulates production environments accurately.
scenes.image_prompt: graph TD; A(Docker) --> B(Images); A --> C(Containers); A --> D(Dockerfile); A --> E(Docker Hub); B --> F(Build from Dockerfile); C --> G(Run Applications); D --> F; E --> B;
